title: Function
statement:
Let $A$ and $B$ be sets. A \emph{function} from $A$ to $B$ is a relation
$f \subseteq A \times B$ such that:
- [label=(\roman*)]
- (\emph{Existence / left-total}) for every $a \in A$, there exists $b \in B$
 with $(a,b) \in f$;
- (\emph{Uniqueness / right-unique}) if $(a,b_1) \in f$ and $(a,b_2) \in f$
 then $b_1 = b_2$.
If $(a,b) \in f$ we write $f(a) = b$.

current_dependencies.title: Cartesian Product — as foundation for relations
